title: In first meeting, federal autism committee focuses on ‘profound autism’
summary: The committee met for the first time since Kennedy fired most of the committee’s scientific experts and replaced them with activists and advocates.

title: STAT+: Health system CEOs get off easy at Congressional hearing on affordability
summary: Committee members largely blamed the other party’s health care policies for driving U.S. health care prices to levels inaccessible to many Americans.

title: STAT+: AIDS group sues Trump administration over undisclosed agreement with Gilead
summary: The AIDS advocacy group PrEP4All has sued the Trump administration over an undisclosed agreement with Gilead.

title: STAT+: FDA launches effort to speed up clinical trials, using AI
summary: Monitoring trials in real time may help shorten the time interval between trial phases.

title: Opinion: Healthcare or health care? Help STAT decide
summary: Should STAT use “healthcare” or “health care”? We want to hear from you.

title: FDA Announces Major Steps to Implement Real-Time Clinical Trials
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced two major steps as part of an initiative to advance the implementation of real-time clinical trials (RTCT).

title: Opinion: FDA commissioner: ‘Smarter,’ real-time clinical trials could transform drug development
summary: “For the first time, FDA regulators will be able to see what’s happening in a clinical trial, looking at endpoints in the cloud as they occur,” writes FDA commissioner.

title: Fauci adviser David Morens indicted on charges of concealing emails, avoiding records requests
summary: The charges against Morens come after Congressional probes related to the origins of the virus that causes Covid-19.

title: Pfizer deals extend patent life for a top-selling rare disease drug
summary: Settlements delaying the arrival of generic versions of tafamidis will protect billions in Pfizer revenue and have implications for others, including BridgeBio Pharma and Alnylam.

title: Boehringer dual-acting obesity shot hits mark in Phase 3 trial
summary: Survodutide spurred weight loss Wall Street analysts referred to as “Wegovy-like,” and showed signs it might help preserve muscle mass as well.

title: STAT+: Supreme Court to hear case about ‘skinny labeling’ and generic access
summary: The Supreme Court Wednesday will hear oral arguments about a simmering controversy over so-called skinny labels, with implications for the availability of generics.

title: FDA claims Amgen drug data were ‘manipulated’; Erasca slides despite ‘home run’ results
summary: Tavneos’ original developer hid a study analysis and then altered data, the FDA alleged. Elsewhere, Erasca’s RAS drug was met with skepticism and Rocket Pharma sold a voucher.

title: STAT+: Boehringer’s obesity drug shows 16.6% weight loss; more data needed
summary: Eli Lilly's deal with Profluent, Erasca's RAS-targeting cancer pill, and more biotech news today

title: STAT+: Google’s clinical director on AI challenges
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech newsletter: a conversation with Google's clinical director, an update on Doctronic's Utah experiment, and more.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about warnings over Trump’s MFN plan, Purdue Pharma sentencing, and more
summary: The Novartis CEO warned that Trump's drug pricing policy poses a “very difficult situation” and the reality will soon catch up with drugmakers and patients

title: Coultreon banks $125M to support testing of former Galapagos immune drug
summary: Backed by Novo Holdings, Regeneron’s venture arm and several others, the biotech believes an oral drug licensed from Galapagos might hold potential treating many immunological diseases.

title: Predicting consequences of new hepatitis B vaccine recs
summary: New data on public trust in science, AI and mental health, and other news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly enlists AI startup for next-generation gene editors
summary: Eli Lilly's deal with Profluent aims to go beyond CRISPR by using AI-designed enzymes to insert entire genes. It could reshape genetic medicine.

title: Biotech investors gamble on M&A. Some drugmakers are betting on themselves instead.
summary: Young biotechs usually get acquired instead of profitably selling their own medicines. A growing number are proving they can, which could make the sector more broadly appealing to investors, some say.

title: STAT+: Google clinical director says AI can be a ‘bridge’ for people having a mental health crisis
summary: Google's clinical director Megan Bell Jones on how the company is addressing concerns over mental health impacts of its AI products.

title: STAT+: A giant question looms over GOP’s new Medicaid work requirements
summary: Coverage losses tied to Medicaid work requirements may not be tracked in every states.

title: Opinion: Why do discussions about ‘brain health’ ignore mental illness?
summary: “Failing to adopt a more inclusive concept of brain health represents a massive loss of human potential,” write Husseini K. Manji, Eric J. Nestler, and Patrick J. Kennedy.

title: Opinion: It’s time to seize the rare bipartisan opening to address microplastics
summary: “This could be the start of serious action on microplastics. Or it could end up as a headline with little behind it,” write Tim Ryan and Justin Zorn.

title: AhR inhibition promotes axon regeneration
Remodelling complex macrocycles
IL-2-TGFβ co-agonist induces immune tolerance
Fast-acting covalent protein drugs
Contemporary design of small-molecule kinase modulators: orthosteric, allosteric and induced-proximity strategies
New U.S. recommendation on hepatitis B vaccine will have dire consequences, studies project
summary: Policy that recommends offering hep B vaccine at birth only to babies perceived to be at risk will lead to increased numbers of infected infants, new studies project.

title: Supreme Court grapples with multibillion-dollar wave of lawsuits over Roundup cancer claims
summary: The Supreme Court seemed divided Monday over whether to block thousands of lawsuits alleging the maker of the weedkiller Roundup failed to warn people it could cause cancer.

title: STAT+: Erasca touts strong, though preliminary, results in trial of pancreatic and lung cancer therapy
summary: The drugmaker Erasca said results on its experimental pill in a trial of pancreatic cancer and non-small cell lung cancer exceeded its expectations.

title: Oruka surges as long-acting psoriasis drug shows early promise
summary: A therapy that works similarly to AbbVie’s blockbuster Skyrizi appeared more potent in a mid-stage trial and has the potential to be dosed less frequently.

title: Veradermics soars on positive data for baldness treatment
summary: The results could support the first approval in decades for an oral medicine that can treat male pattern baldness, though the therapy will face competition from generics and over-the-counter…

title: Lilly to buy startup Ajax in bid for a better JAK drug
summary: Worth up to $2.3 billion overall, the deal hands Lilly a drug designed to address the weaknesses of a class of medicines used to treat myelofibrosis and other diseases.

title: Intellia CRISPR drug succeeds in late-stage study against rare swelling disorder
summary: The findings position Intellia to bring to market the first “in vivo” gene editing medicine, though the therapy’s commercial potential remains the source of intense investor debate.

title: In vivo CAR T purchase spree continues with Lilly deal worth up to US$7 billion
The evolving landscape of CDK inhibitor use in breast cancer therapy and beyond
Sanofi MS drug rejected in US gets an endorsement in Europe
summary: Tolebrutinib, which the FDA recently turned back due to safety concerns, received a positive recommendation that positions the therapy for its first regulatory approval.

title: FDA Accelerates Action on Treatments for Serious Mental Illness Following Executive Order
summary: On April 18, President Trump issued an Executive Order directing the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to accelerate access to treatments for patients with serious mental illness,…

title: Inhibiting the integrated stress response restores cognition
FDA Approves First-Ever Gene Therapy for Treatment of Genetic Hearing Loss Under National Priority Voucher Program
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Otarmeni (lunsotogene parvec-cwha), the first-ever dual adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector-based gene therapy.

title: CMS and FDA Announce RAPID Coverage Pathway to Accelerate Patient Access to Life-Changing Medical Devices
summary: CMS and FDA announce the Regulatory Alignment for Predictable and Immediate Device (RAPID) coverage pathway, a new pathway designed to expedite access to certain FDA-designated Class II and Class III…
